Build your milkshake empire

Blend shakes by hand, hire staff so the cash keeps coming while you're away, and grow a tiny stand into a busy chain.

Click the cup to blend!

0blends

How it plays

Simple to start, deep enough to lose hours to.

Tap to blend

Hit the cup to make a shake. Chain quick blends to stack combos and cash in bigger.

Hire blenders

Your crew keeps the shakes flowing while you're gone. They earn while you sleep.

Upgrade and expand

Pour your earnings into faster gear and new shops. Watch one stand turn into a chain.

Pro tips

Get blending like a tycoon.

Hire first

Get staff blending before you splurge on upgrades. The sooner they start, the faster your money snowballs.

Chase combos

Quick clicks in a row build a blend multiplier. Keep the streak going for the biggest payouts.

Earn while idle

Log off with a full crew hired and come back to a fat pile of stacked-up cash.

Gameplay

A peek at the milkshake empire in action.

Frequently asked questions (FAQ)

Is it really free?

Yes — completely free, with no ads and no purchases. It's a passion project.

Do I need to install anything?

No. It runs right in your browser. A Windows launcher is also available if you'd prefer a desktop app with offline play and auto-updates.

Does my progress save?

Yes. The game auto-saves to your browser, so you can close the tab and pick up right where you left off.

What is the launcher for?

It's an optional desktop app that manages game versions, lets you play offline, and keeps both the game and itself up to date automatically.

What platforms can I play on?

Any modern browser on desktop or mobile. The standalone launcher is Windows-only.

How do I report a bug or suggest an idea?

Head to the GitHub repo and open an issue — feedback is always welcome.

Do I need an account?

Nope — no sign-up, no login. Just open the game and start blending. Your progress lives right in your browser.

Can I play on mobile?

Yes. The layout is touch-friendly and works on phones and tablets — tap to blend, just like clicking on desktop. Important: The GUI in v1.0 and lower is known to not adapt well to smaller screens, with overlapping elements, etc. Using v1.1 (when released, at the time of writing) will solve this. The website automatically uses the latest version.

Are there more updates coming?

It's actively worked on, with new upgrades, mechanics, and balance tweaks rolling out. Watch the releases page to keep up.

Could clearing my browser data wipe my save?

Yes — the save is stored locally, so clearing site data resets it. The desktop launcher keeps things tidier if you want a more permanent setup.

How do I earn money faster?

Stack upgrades for a bigger payout per blend, then hire employees so the cash keeps flowing even when you're not clicking.

What are special outcomes?

Lucky blends — Crusty, Creamy, Decorated Baked, Swirled, Golden, and more — that pay bonus cash. Rarer ones pay far more, and effects can stack.

Can blending be automated?

Yes. You can enable Auto Blend mode using button in the top left corner. You can also buy upgrades to reduce blend time (applies for auto and manual clicking).

How do I reset my progress?

Open Settings in-game and use the hard reset option. It wipes your save and starts you fresh — there's no undo, so be sure.

Can I back up or move my save?

Settings includes export and import, so you can save a backup file or carry your progress to another device.

Is there an end goal?

It's open-ended — keep upgrading, hiring, and expanding to grow your empire as large as you like. New content arrives over time.

Why is the launcher Windows-only?

The desktop launcher is a Windows app. On any other system, the browser version plays exactly the same — no install needed. The real reason why the launcher is only on Windows is because I [Strider657] use a Windows PC.

Does the launcher cost anything?

No — the launcher is free too. It just adds offline play, version management, and automatic updates.

How do I get the latest version?

The browser game is always current. The launcher updates both itself and the game automatically, or grab builds from the releases page.

I found a bug — what now?

Open an issue on GitHub with what happened. Reproduction steps and screenshots help a ton.

Ready to blend?

Your first milkshake is one click away.
